title: Effect variable ?E not in type parameter list — third generic mechanism
description: Ferrum has three distinct syntactic mechanisms for generics: (1) type parameters in [T, U] brackets; (2) region/lifetime parameters in ['a] or <'a, 'b> (spec is inconsistent); (3) effect variables ?E declared by use anywhere in an effect position. A caller learning generics must master three different parameter mechanisms. Effect variables are especially unusual: they are 'declared by use' (no declaration site, just a ?-prefixed name in an effect annotation), and they are always inferred, never explicit at call sites. This is a novel concept with no analogue in any mainstream language. The spec should either: (a) unify effect variables into the [T] parameter list (e.g., fn map[T, U, ?E](f: fn(T): U ! ?E)), making the parameter explicit and visible; or (b) justify why a third mechanism is necessary and document it prominently in the generics chapter.
